title: build ARGs are not accessible in `ENV` when building with Dockerfile.
body:
**Description**

build ARGs are not accessible in `ENV` when building with Dockerfile.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create Dockerfile.

   ```dockerfile
   FROM ubuntu
   ARG CONT_IMG_VER
   ENV CONT_IMG_VER=${CONT_IMG_VER:-v1.0.0}
   RUN echo $CONT_IMG_VER
   ```

2. Build image.

   ```shell
   buildah build --build-arg CONT_IMG_VER=xyz -t test .
   ```

**Describe the results you received:**

`echo $CONT_IMG_VER` outputs `v1.0.0`

**Describe the results you expected:**

`echo $CONT_IMG_VER` outputs `xyz`

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/unknown,now 1.28.2-0ubuntu22.04+obs37.1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.28.2
Go Version:      go1.18.1
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.23.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.1
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=22.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=jammy
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 22.04.1 LTS"
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 22.04.1 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="22.04"
VERSION="22.04.1 LTS (Jammy Jellyfish)"
VERSION_CODENAME=jammy
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
UBUNTU_CODENAME=jammy
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux aarons 5.15.0-58-generic #64-Ubuntu SMP Thu Jan 5 11:43:13 UTC 2023 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
cat: /etc/containers/storage.conf: No such file or directory
```

comments.body:
Hi @aaronjheng , Following bug does not persists in the latest version

```console
[fl@fedora bin]$ ./buildah build --build-arg CONT_IMG_VER=xyz -t test .
STEP 1/4: FROM alpine
STEP 2/4: ARG CONT_IMG_VER
STEP 3/4: ENV CONT_IMG_VER=${CONT_IMG_VER:-v1.0.0}
STEP 4/4: RUN echo $CONT_IMG_VER
xyz
COMMIT test
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 8e012198eea1 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob b8df725f7381 done  
Copying config c95c67e01f done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> c95c67e01fa
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
c95c67e01faadba5829a223d12bdc3c79b63b1215c744a8d603a8e8fbc20fc0d
```

Could you please try with latest version and report back :) I am closing this issue but please feel free to re-open if something is unresolved.

Sorry. Wrong Dockerfile. 
The original one which should be reported is:
```dockerfile
ARG CONT_IMG_VER
FROM ubuntu
ENV CONT_IMG_VER=${CONT_IMG_VER:-v1.0.0}
RUN echo $CONT_IMG_VER
```
Then I realized `CONT_IMG_VER` is not in next stage's scope.  So the Dockerfile in issue description is right.
Thanks!
@aaronjheng Thanks following PR should close this https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/242
Sorry. My last response is not clear enough. I mean buildah's behavior is totally fine. The Dockerfile is problematic.
